description_long: This object is a classic portable stereo system, commonly known as a boombox, featuring two large detachable speakers on either side of a central control unit. The front panel is equipped with a variety of buttons, dials, and sliders for audio control, as well as a cassette deck and a CD tray, indicating its multi-format playback capability. A sturdy handle is mounted on top for easy carrying, and the speakers have prominent circular grilles with visible woofers and tweeters. The rear side reveals multiple input/output ports and battery compartments, suggesting both AC and battery-powered operation. The exterior is primarily constructed from black plastic with some metallic accents, giving it a robust and utilitarian appearance. This type of device is often found in urban environments, used for playing music in public spaces, parks, or garages.
description: A black, portable boombox with large detachable speakers, a central control panel with buttons and dials, and a top handle for carrying. It features a CD tray, cassette deck, and multiple audio controls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speakers, control panel, CD tray, cassette deck, and various buttons and dials.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the speaker and the rectangular profile of the unit.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying input/output ports, battery compartments, and the back of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the other speaker's profile.
